mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals aggregate income formation and consumption capacity; positive payroll changes predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month PAYEMS changes are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 7.0 percent indicates substantial labor market slack that depresses wage-driven consumption and corporate pricing power; this threshold predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 7.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between industrial production growth and retail sales growth captures the supply-demand loop strength; high interaction values predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days because synchronized expansion lifts corporate revenue and margins.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and RSAFS fails to produce a positive rank correlation with 20-day S&P 500 returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During high-unemployment regimes, month-over-month declines in retail sales signal collapsing household demand and trigger rapid risk-off repositioning in equities over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6.0% and RSAFS declines month-over-month, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1.1 million units signal insufficient residential investment to sustain cyclical momentum, predicting negative 60-day S&P 500 returns through weaker construction and durable goods demand.
failure_condition: When HOUST falls below 1100, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 3.0 percent annualized signals monetary policy reversal risk; this threshold predicts negative 5-day S&P 500 returns as forward rate expectations reprice higher.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3.0%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between industrial production momentum and the federal funds rate captures the cost of capital for goods-producing firms; rising production alongside rising policy rates predicts negative 20-day S&P 500 returns via margin compression.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and FEDFUNDS fails to produce a negative rank correlation with 20-day S&P 500 returns (Rank IC > -0.03) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP predicts positive 60-day S&P 500 returns because aggregate output expansion drives broad-based revenue growth and positive earnings surprises across the index.
failure_condition: Positive quarterly GDPC1 growth is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.
